summary: Caldrith is a multi-tenant GitHub App (FastAPI ingest + an ARQ/Redis worker, NOT a CLI or Marketplace Action) that enforces GitHub configuration-as-code modelled on github/safe-settings: install the App, create an admin repo with .github/settings.yml, and Caldrith reconciles repository settings to match. The HTTP ingest path is thin (verify raw-body HMAC -> dedup on X-GitHub-Delivery -> enqueue ARQ job -> 202); heavy reconcile runs in the worker, fanning out one job per repo for full-account syncs. `config/` (schema + loader + diff) is the pure, I/O-free core. P1 enforces ONLY the repository: block's three merge fields (allow_auto_merge, delete_branch_on_merge, allow_update_branch) end-to-end, on github.com Organization AND User accounts.

settings.purpose: pydantic-settings AppConfig reading env: APP_ID, PRIVATE_KEY (PEM), WEBHOOK_SECRET, REDIS_URL, GITHUB_API_URL (default https://api.github.com), ADMIN_REPO (default 'admin'), CONFIG_PATH (default '.github'), SETTINGS_FILE_PATH (default 'settings.yml').

api.app.purpose: create_app() FastAPI factory; mounts the webhooks router and ratelimit; GET /healthz (no deps), GET /readyz (Redis reachable).

api.webhooks.purpose: POST /: read RAW body -> verify signature -> dedup on X-GitHub-Delivery -> enqueue ARQ job -> return 202. Routes push/repository/pull_request to the right job. THIN — no reconcile here.

api.security.purpose: verify_signature(secret, body, sig256_header) -> bool using HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW body with hmac.compare_digest. Pure, I/O-free.

api.ratelimit.purpose: slowapi limiter (per-IP + per-installation), Redis-backed with an in-memory fallback.

auth.client.purpose: GitHubClientFactory.for_installation(installation_id, base_url) -> a githubkit GitHub client using AppInstallationAuthStrategy. Builds a NEW client per job; never shares tokens across installations; base_url configurable for GHES.

config.schema.purpose: PURE. RepositorySettings (the 3 required P1 fields + common optional safe-settings fields) and SafeSettingsConfig(repository: RepositorySettings | None, ...stub deferred tiers). model_json_schema() usable for docs.

config.loader.purpose: load_admin_config(client, owner, admin_repo, config_path, settings_file, ref=None) -> SafeSettingsConfig: fetch file contents via the GitHub API, yaml.safe_load, validate into the pydantic model.

config.diff.purpose: PURE. compare_deep(actual, desired) -> Diff(additions, modifications, deletions, has_changes). Ports safe-settings compareDeep semantics; ignores keys containing 'url' and the set {id, node_id}. I/O-free, idempotency-tested.

reconcile.planner.purpose: List target repos for an installation (account-type aware: Organization vs User) and build a per-repo reconcile plan.

reconcile.repository.purpose: RepositoryApplier: given desired RepositorySettings + a repo, compute the diff vs live settings and apply via repos.update (or return a NopResult in dry-run / when there are no changes).

reconcile.runner.purpose: run_reconcile(installation_id, owner, repos=None, dry_run=False, head_sha=None): orchestrate load_admin_config -> plan -> apply/nop. In dry_run, post a GitHub Check Run summarising the diff and mutate nothing.

worker.worker.purpose: ARQ WorkerSettings + job funcs reconcile_installation and reconcile_repo; Redis settings derived from REDIS_URL.

worker.queue.purpose: Enqueue helpers + dedup_delivery(redis, delivery_id) using Redis SETNX with a TTL to drop duplicate webhook deliveries.

audit.logging.purpose: structlog JSON config + a helper to bind installation_id / delivery_id / repo onto the logger. Never binds credentials.

note_for_agents: Load this file on demand to locate code; it is NOT auto-imported into CLAUDE.md. Design rule: config/ (schema + loader + diff) stays pure — no githubkit, ARQ, FastAPI, or network imports — so the schema and diff are unit-tested with synthetic dicts. Ingest contract: POST / verifies the HMAC over the RAW body BEFORE any parse, dedups on X-GitHub-Delivery, enqueues, and returns 202 — no reconcile in the request. Build a NEW githubkit client per installation (AppInstallationAuthStrategy); never share tokens; base_url is configurable for the GHES future. P1 reconciles ONLY repository.{allow_auto_merge, delete_branch_on_merge, allow_update_branch}; PR events are dry-run (Check Run) only. Deferred (clean seams, not implemented): branch protection, rulesets, labels, teams, collaborators, environments, custom properties, suborg/repo overlay tiers, GHES multi-registration, CEL policy. Tests mirror modules 1:1 under tests/.
